name: Sauteed Catfish with Ginger Peach Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add ginger and stir for 1 minute.
2. If using fresh peaches, add them to the saucepan and cook, stirring occasionally until they are fork-tender (about 2 minutes). Stir in canned peaches, preserves, catsup, vinegar, and hot pepper sauce. Blend until the peaches are coarsely chopped. Cool and refrigerate up to 2 weeks.
3. In a medium bowl or pie plate, mix flour, thyme, salt, and black pepper.
4. Rinse fish and pat dry. Coat each fillet with the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
5. Heat oil and butter in a frying pan over medium heat. Add pecans and cook until fragrant (1-2 minutes).
6. Add fish to the pan without overlapping. Cook for about 4 minutes until crisp and golden on the bottom. Flip and cook another 4 minutes until opaque and tender at the center.
7. Remove fish and drain briefly before serving. Spoon about 3 Tablespoons warm Ginger Peach Sauce over each serving and sprinkle with pecans.
8. Makes 4 servings.
(Note: This recipe is from Simply Seafood Magazine - Winter 1991)
